primary_meaning: a road in a city or town
usage_notes: Usually has buildings on both sides
common_collocations: main street, street light, cross the street
image_search_query: busy city street with cars and buildings
image_context: A busy city street with cars, buildings, and pedestrians walking on the sidewalk
memory_hook: You 'street' through the city on roads

primary_meaning: a public garden or field for recreation
secondary_meaning: to leave a car in a place
usage_notes: Where people go to relax and play
common_collocations: public park, park bench, go to the park
image_search_query: green park with trees and bench
image_context: A beautiful green public park with tall trees, a bench, and people walking on the path
example_1: The children are playing in the park.
example_1_translation: As crianças estão brincando no parque.
example_2: Let's go to the park today.
memory_hook: You 'park' yourself to relax in the park
common_mistakes: Don't confuse noun 'park' (place) with verb 'park' (to stop car)

primary_meaning: a sandy area next to the sea
usage_notes: Where people swim and sunbathe
common_collocations: sandy beach, beach ball, go to the beach
image_search_query: sandy beach with ocean waves and umbrella
image_context: A sandy beach with blue ocean waves, colorful umbrellas, and people swimming
example_1: I want to go to the beach.
example_3: We swim in the sea at the beach.
example_3_translation: Nós nadamos no mar na praia.
memory_hook: You 'beach' yourself on the sandy shore

primary_meaning: a long flow of water to the sea
usage_notes: Fresh water flowing to the ocean
common_collocations: river bank, river water, cross the river
image_search_query: long river flowing through green landscape
image_context: A long river flowing peacefully through a green landscape with trees on its banks
example_2: We can swim in the river.
memory_hook: A river 'rivets' the land together as it flows

primary_meaning: an area where flowers and plants grow
usage_notes: Usually at the back of a house
common_collocations: front garden, garden flower, garden gate
image_search_query: beautiful garden with colorful flowers
image_context: A beautiful garden with colorful flowers, green grass, and a white garden gate
example_1: We grow flowers in the garden.
memory_hook: Gardens are where 'guard'ians watch the plants

primary_meaning: a structure built over a river or road
usage_notes: Connects two places separated by water or land
common_collocations: long bridge, bridge over water, cross the bridge
image_search_query: long stone bridge over river
image_context: A long stone bridge crossing over a calm river with boats underneath
example_1: We cross the bridge to go to school.
example_1_translation: Atravessamos a ponte para ir à escola.
memory_hook: A bridge 'bridges' the gap between two places

primary_meaning: a very high hill
usage_notes: Higher than hills, often with snow on top
common_collocations: high mountain, mountain top, climb the mountain
image_search_query: tall mountain with snow on top
image_context: A tall mountain with white snow on its peak and green trees at its base
memory_hook: Mountains 'mount' up to the sky

primary_meaning: a person who teaches in school
usage_notes: Someone who helps students learn
common_collocations: good teacher, English teacher, teacher and student
image_search_query: teacher standing in classroom with students
image_context: A teacher standing at the front of a classroom pointing to the board while students listen
memory_hook: A teacher 'teaches' you knowledge

primary_meaning: a person who studies at school
usage_notes: Someone who is learning
common_collocations: good student, English student, student and teacher
image_search_query: young student reading book in library
image_context: A young student sitting at a desk reading a book in a quiet library
example_2_translation: O estudante está fazendo a lição de casa.
example_3: Many students are in the class.
memory_hook: Students are 'studying' to learn

primary_meaning: one side of a sheet of paper
usage_notes: Part of a book or document
common_collocations: book page, page number, turn the page
image_search_query: open book showing white pages with text
image_context: An open book showing clean white pages with black text and numbers at the bottom
example_1_translation: Por favor, leia a página dez.
memory_hook: You 'page' through a book turning pages

primary_meaning: a written message sent to someone
usage_notes: Can be written message or alphabet character
common_collocations: love letter, receive a letter, write a letter
image_search_query: white envelope with letter inside
image_context: A white envelope with a red stamp, ready to be mailed with a letter inside
example_1: I wrote a letter to my friend.
example_1_translation: Eu escrevi uma carta para meu amigo.
example_2: The letter is on the table.
example_3_translation: Por favor, me envie uma carta.
memory_hook: You 'let' letters out when you write them

primary_meaning: a single unit of language
usage_notes: Building block of sentences
common_collocations: new word, English word, word meaning
image_search_query: hand writing word on paper
image_context: A hand writing the word 'HELLO' on a white piece of paper with a pen
example_2: I learned a new word today.
example_2_translation: Eu aprendi uma palavra nova hoje.
memory_hook: Words 'word' your thoughts together

primary_meaning: a group of words forming a complete thought
secondary_meaning: a punishment given by a judge
usage_notes: Starts with capital letter, ends with period
common_collocations: complete sentence, long sentence, write a sentence
image_search_query: sentence written on notebook paper
image_context: A notebook paper with a complete sentence written in blue ink: 'I love learning English.'
memory_hook: A sentence 'sends' a complete thought
common_mistakes: A sentence must have subject and verb to be complete

primary_meaning: a sentence asking for information
usage_notes: Usually ends with a question mark
common_collocations: good question, answer the question, ask a question
image_search_query: question mark on white paper
image_context: A large red question mark drawn on white paper with a question written below it
example_2: What is the answer to this question?
example_2_translation: Qual é a resposta para esta pergunta?
memory_hook: You 'quest' for answers with questions

primary_meaning: what you say when someone asks something
usage_notes: Comes after a question
common_collocations: right answer, wrong answer, find the answer
image_search_query: person writing answer on paper
image_context: A person writing the correct answer on a test paper with a pencil
memory_hook: You 'answer' when called to respond
common_mistakes: 'Answer' starts with 'A' but it's not for vowels - just memorize

primary_meaning: the ongoing progression of moments
secondary_meaning: a specific point in the day
usage_notes: Uncountable noun, use 'some time' or 'a lot of time'
common_collocations: what time, free time, have time
image_search_query: clock showing time on white background
image_context: A round clock showing 3 o'clock with the minute hand pointing to twelve
memory_hook: Time 'times' all our activities
common_mistakes: Uncountable - never say 'a time' for time in general

primary_meaning: sixty minutes
usage_notes: Used to measure duration
common_collocations: one hour, half an hour, per hour
image_search_query: clock showing one hour passing
image_context: A clock face showing the minute hand moved from 12 to 6, representing half an hour
example_1: I'll be there in one hour.
example_2: There are twenty-four hours in a day.
example_2_translation: Há vinte e quatro horas em um dia.
example_3: The movie is two hours long.
example_3_translation: O filme tem duas horas de duração.
memory_hook: Each hour 'owers' us sixty minutes
common_mistakes: Starts with vowel sound - use 'an hour' not 'a hour'

primary_meaning: seven days
usage_notes: Monday to Sunday is one week
common_collocations: this week, last week, next week
image_search_query: calendar showing week with days marked
image_context: A calendar page showing seven days highlighted in blue to represent one week
example_2: There are seven days in a week.
example_2_translation: Há sete dias em uma semana.
memory_hook: A 'week' 'weeks' by slowly

primary_meaning: a period of about thirty days
usage_notes: January, February, March are months
common_collocations: this month, last month, per month
image_search_query: calendar showing month view with dates
image_context: A calendar showing a full month view with all thirty days marked and today circled
example_1: There are twelve months in a year.
example_1_translation: Há doze meses em um ano.
example_2_translation: Meu aniversário é no próximo mês.
example_3: I visit my mother once a month.
example_3_translation: Eu visito minha mãe uma vez por mês.
pronunciation_tips: Short 'u' sound, rhymes with 'sun' but ends with 'th'
memory_hook: The 'month' 'moons' over us every month
common_mistakes: 'Month' not 'munth' - pronounce the 'th' at the end

primary_meaning: this current day
usage_notes: Refers to the day we are in now
common_collocations: today morning, today afternoon, today evening
image_search_query: calendar with today circled in red
image_context: A calendar showing today's date circled in red marker on the current day of the week
memory_hook: To-DAY means 'the day' we are in now
common_mistakes: 'Today' has no capital letter when used in sentences

primary_meaning: the day after today
usage_notes: Refers to the next day
common_collocations: tomorrow morning, tomorrow afternoon, see you tomorrow
image_search_query: calendar showing tomorrow's date circled
image_context: A calendar with tomorrow's date circled in green, showing the day after today
memory_hook: To-morrow is 'more' of today, coming again

primary_meaning: the day before today
usage_notes: Refers to the past day
common_collocations: yesterday morning, yesterday afternoon, yesterday evening
image_search_query: calendar showing yesterday's date marked
image_context: A calendar showing yesterday's date with an X marked on it in grey
example_2: I went to the park yesterday.
example_3: Did you see the movie yesterday?
memory_hook: Yes-ter-day - 'yes' to the past day that already was

primary_meaning: moving at high speed
usage_notes: Opposite of slow
common_collocations: run fast, fast car, fast food
image_search_query: fast race car on track speeding
image_context: A bright red race car speeding fast on a race track with motion blur behind it
memory_hook: When you 'fast', you go quickly without stopping
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'fast' (speed) with 'fast' (not eating) - same word different meaning

primary_meaning: moving at low speed
usage_notes: Opposite of fast
common_collocations: very slow, slow car, slow learner
image_search_query: slow moving turtle on green grass
image_context: A slow moving brown turtle crawling on green grass in a garden
memory_hook: Go 'slow' and take it 'slow' to get there

primary_meaning: free from dirt
secondary_meaning: to make something free from dirt
usage_notes: Opposite of dirty
common_collocations: very clean, clean water, clean room
image_search_query: clean white bathroom with shiny faucet
image_context: A clean white bathroom with a shiny silver faucet and sparkling clean mirror
memory_hook: Clean things 'lean' without dirt
